title: Boat Rental in Mafia Island: A 2026 Guide to Costs and Cruising
html:
Renting a yacht in Mafia Island opens up the turquoise waters of the Mafia Archipelago, with departures from Kilindoni Harbour and prices from €250 per day. BoatHire24 connects you with verified skippers and a choice of motor yachts, catamarans and ribs to explore this Indian Ocean gem.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Mafia Island?

Day rates for a private motorboat start at about €250 and can reach €1,200 for a fully crewed catamaran. Prices depend on boat size, duration and season. A shared snorkelling tour costs from €60 per person. For the best choice and transparent pricing, browse the yacht charter Mafia Island listings on BoatHire24, where every boat has verified availability and inclusive fees.

Where to set off in Mafia Island

Most charters depart from Kilindoni, the main port on the island. Other popular departure points include Bweni village on the north coast, the jetty at Chole Bay, and the anchorage off Ras Mkumbi. The nearby islands of Chole, Jibondo and Juani are common destinations, all reachable within a 30-minute cruise. The marine park headquarters at Utende also offer moorings for longer trips.

Which boat to rent

  1. Rib or speedboat, ideal for day trips to the marine park, up to 8 guests, from €250 half-day.
  2. Motor yacht (e.g. Olympic 490), comfortable for offshore cruising, up to 10 guests, from €450 per day. See the Olympic 490 listing for full specs and availability.
  3. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 40), stable and spacious for families, up to 12 guests, from €800 full-day.
  4. Traditional dhow, authentic sailing experience, up to 6 guests, from €200 half-day (often skippered).

Costs for a day on the water

  • Shared snorkelling tour: €60, €100 per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (up to 6 guests): €250, €400.
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 12 guests): €800, €1,200.
  • Crewed yacht with meals (up to 10 guests): from €1,500 per day.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Mafia Island?

For bareboat rental of a motor yacht over 15 knots, an International Certificate of Competence or equivalent is generally required. Most charter operators on Mafia Island require a licence for larger vessels. Skippered charters are widely available and are recommended for visitors unfamiliar with local reefs and currents. The operator confirms exact requirements at the time of booking.

Visit the Mafia Island Marine Park between June and October when the kaskazi wind brings calm seas and excellent visibility.

When is the best season for boat rental in Mafia Island?

The high season runs from June to October, with reliable south-east trade winds and sunny weather. During these months sea conditions are at their calmest, especially around the marine park. The low season (November to May) has higher rainfall and more variable winds, but offers lower prices and fewer crowds. Peak charter demand occurs in August and over Christmas.

At a glance

  • Price band: €250, €1,200 per day (private boat)
  • Peak months: June to October
  • Trip length: half-day to full-week charters
  • Capacity: 6, 12 guests per boat
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Mafia Island

What is included in the rental price?

Most inclusive packages cover the boat, fuel, skipper, snorkelling gear and basic refreshments.

Can I book a multi-day charter around the archipelago?

Yes, several operators offer 3, 7 day itineraries visiting Chole, Jibondo and the marine park.

Are there any age restrictions for passengers?

Children of all ages are welcome on most boats, but lifejackets must be available for all on board.

How far is it from Kilindoni to the marine park?

The main reserve area at Chole Bay is about 8 nautical miles north-east of Kilindoni Harbour.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Mafia Island and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
